mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that accelerates corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in real sales (RSAFS) above 425 billion dollars signals strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 425 billion.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.3579 signals weakening supply chain capacity that reduces inflationary pressures, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below 99.3579.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 15,679.7 billion dollars signals robust economic growth that enhances corporate earnings prospects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 15,679.7 billion.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 233.526 combined with stable FEDFUNDS signals persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain tight policy, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of CPIAUCSL > 233.526 and FEDFUNDS stability fails to produce negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 891 thousand units signals a recovery in the construction sector that boosts economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 891 thousand.
